pattern: XOR each standard Gray-code value with the start offset to rotate the sequence so it begins at start while preserving the single-bit-change property.
hints: The n-bit Gray code produces a Hamiltonian cycle over all 2^n integers where consecutive entries differ by exactly one bit.
Rotating a Gray-code sequence to begin at start is the same as XORing every element with start, since XOR distributes uniformly over the sequence.
Generate the i-th Gray code with i ^ (i >> 1), XOR it with start, and collect all 2^n results starting from i=0.

edge_inputs.description: n=16, start=0 — largest size starting at zero
start is not a power of two — tests non-trivial XOR shift

stress.description: random n in [1,12] with random start
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 12))
    start = rng.randint(0, (1 << n) - 1)
    return [n, start]
